name: Cervical Spine CT Without Contrast
description: Standard cervical spine CT report template without contrast for evaluation of bony anatomy

findings.technique: CT of the cervical spine was performed without contrast in axial and sagittal reconstructions.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Vertebral Bodies
sections.content: The cervical vertebral bodies from C1 through C7 demonstrate [normal height / no compression fracture / no bony lesion]. The bone density is [normal / no lytic or blastic lesion].

sections.name: Vertebral Artery Grooves
sections.content: The vertebral artery grooves are [intact / no abnormal configuration]. The transverse foramina are [patent / symmetric].

sections.name: Pedicles
sections.content: The bilateral pedicles are [intact / no fracture / no lytic lesion]. No pedicle thinning or erosion.

sections.name: Laminae
sections.content: The laminae are [intact / no fracture / no bony defect]. No laminar fracture.

sections.name: Spinous Processes
sections.content: The spinous processes are [intact / no fracture / no non-union]. No abnormal calcification.

sections.name: Facet Joints
sections.content: The facet joints demonstrate [no malalignment / no subluxation / no degenerative changes]. The joint spaces are [preserved / normal].

sections.name: Intervertebral Disc Spaces
sections.content: The intervertebral disc spaces are [preserved / normal height]. No disc space narrowing or vacuum phenomenon.

sections.name: Spinal Canal
sections.content: The spinal canal is [adequate in diameter / no bony stenosis]. No posterior element hypertrophy causing canal compromise.

sections.name: Neural Foramina
sections.content: The bilateral neural foramina are [patent / adequate in size / no bony narrowing]. No foraminal stenosis.

sections.name: Soft Tissues
sections.content: The prevertebral and paraspinal soft tissues are [unremarkable / no abnormal soft tissue mass]. No abnormal soft tissue swelling.

impression:
1. Normal cervical spine CT.
2. No fracture, dislocation, or bony abnormality identified.
author: RadReport Templates
